name: Tomato Lentil Stew Over Quinoa
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium-large saucepan, bring water to a boil and add washed lentils. Cook for about 30-40 minutes, or until the lentils are thoroughly cooked.
2. While the lentils cook, chop onions and tomatoes.
3. In a very large skillet or pan, sauté onions over low heat until they are mostly translucent.
4. Add tomatoes to the skillet and stir occasionally.
5. Crush marjoram and savory in your palm and rub together to release flavor. Add the herbs and salt to the skillet.
6. Once the tomatoes appear stewy and cooked, add the cooked, well-drained lentils. Stir to combine and cook for a few minutes.
7. To prepare quinoa, bring 2 parts water to 1 part quinoa in a separate pot. Cook according to package instructions (usually boiling, then lowering to a simmer).
8. Once the quinoa is ready, stir it into the stew and let sit off the heat for ten minutes before serving.
9. For an extra touch, sauté a handful of soaked arame seaweed with a little oil, garlic, and ginger in a small pan until crispy. Sprinkle over the stew or any pasta dish.
